title: Briar House
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: An archivist cataloguing a crumbling estate discovers the house's dark history is not past — it is waiting.
long_description: Briar House has stood empty for forty years, its rooms sealed, its gardens overgrown, its secrets intact. When archivist Clara March arrives to catalogue the estate's library, she finds a house that does not want her there: doors that open to the wrong rooms, mirrors that show reflections that do not match, and a child's laugh echoing from a nursery that has been locked since 1923. The Briar family history is written in blood and silence, and Clara is the first person in a generation to read it. A slow-burn gothic horror about inheritance, memory, and the things that live in the walls of the houses we refuse to leave.
